title: Build a Controlled Form with Validation
prompt:
Build a `SignupForm` component with the following fields: `name` (required, min 2 chars), `email` (required, valid email format), and `password` (required, min 8 chars).

Requirements:
- Use controlled inputs (`value` + `onChange`) for all fields
- Validate each field on blur; show an inline error message below the field
- Disable the submit button when any field has an error or is empty
- On successful submit, display a success message and reset the form
- No external form libraries — plain React state only
context:
**Controlled inputs** are the React standard for form handling: React owns the state, and the DOM reflects it. Every keystroke calls `onChange`, which updates state, which re-renders the input with the new value.

The validation challenge is timing. Validating on every keystroke is distracting — you get an error on the first character of your email before you have finished typing. The conventional UX is **validate on blur**: show errors only after the user has left a field, then re-validate on each change once the field has been touched.

A `touched` record (`{ [field]: boolean }`) is the cleanest way to track this. An error only renders if both `errors[field]` is set and `touched[field]` is true. On submit, mark all fields as touched so validation runs even if the user never focused a field.

resources.title: React – Reacting to Input with State
